summary: A multi-writer room that deepens the コトバ探偵 world: 7 districts as chapters, a recurring cast, the サイレント mystery arc, per-district beats, and in-world ナゾ framing

result.seasonMystery:
SEASON MYSTERY — "だれが、いちばん はじめに、しずかに なったの？ / Who fell silent FIRST?"

CANON (resolves all 4 drafts into one). ソネア is a land MADE of language: prologue physics stays load-bearing — 「こえに だした ことばが、そのまま いろに なる」. The first word was spoken at the centre (灰色のひろば, 準1級) and rippled OUTWARD in 7 rings; so the FARTHEST ring (5級, はじまりの村) holds the OLDEST/simplest words and the CENTRE the youngest/hardest. The player travels edge→centre = 5級→準1級 = the historical order language grew. That is why difficulty and geography already agree in code.

WHO サイレント IS (twist seeded ch.1, named only at 準1級): サイレント is the FIRST SPEAKER — a non-gendered child word-keeper exactly like きみ, named アイラ. (Resolves the two drafts' "Siren/storyteller" and "Ila/first-speaker" into ONE: アイラ, the first storyteller.) Not a villain, not a 魔王, never attacked from outside. アイラ gave the world words for so long, and listened to so many, that they lost their own voice among all the others; people began wounding each other with words (the in-code capital history). Exhausted and unheard, アイラ believed a gentle wrong thing — 「もし わたしが だまれば、だれも きずつかない」 — and stopped speaking. Because アイラ was the SOURCE, their silence drained backward through every ring, un-saying words centre→outward, colour fading district by district. サイレント is a person holding their breath so hard the world forgot to exhale. きみ is アイラ's mirror — who きみ could become if they stopped listening. The "fresh-ink decrees from a King silent 100 years" = アイラ's old written rules running on autopilot while the heart behind them went mute (this inference IS the 準1級 reading payoff).

MACGUFFIN (unify the two competing devices into one): the 7 声の石 (KEEP — already in code, map UI, cleared lines) are reconceived as the 7 fragments of THE FIRST WORD. Each is awarded on clearing a district AS a torn page/line of アイラ's first storybook ("ことばの しおり / Word-Bookmark" is the diegetic skin of the 声の石 — same object, two names reconciled: the STONE is what the map shows, the PAGE is what the story reveals it to be). Reassembled, the 7 lines form アイラ's first sentence: "Once, I told a story, and the whole grey world turned to colour." / 「むかし わたしが おはなしを すると、はいいろの せかいが ぜんぶ いろに なった。」 The finale is not a battle: きみ READS アイラ their own forgotten story back, and returns the LAST word — アイラ's own name. Restoration, not conquest.

MOAT-AS-STORY (the spine that rewards finishing): the 準1級 reading-inference items ARE the mystery's final clues. You cannot understand the ending without the exam skill, so finishing the English IS finishing the story. The deepest secret and the hardest 英検 skill are the same gate — unspoilable by skipping.

PER-CHAPTER CLUE BEATS (one concrete, fair, convergent clue each; all delivered via existing onCorrect / NPC lines, never by altering an answer):
- Ch.1 (5級): おばあさん/灰守セル — 「この しずけさは、そとから きたんじゃない。まんなかから、あるいて きたんだよ。」 (DIRECTION: centre→edge). + Bookmark #1 = "Once, I".
- Ch.2 (4級): reuse Sura's existing line 「サイレントは、しずけさを へいわ だと 思っているらしい」 + gloss "He thinks silence is kindness." (MOTIVE = mercy). Bookmark #2 = "told a story,".
- Ch.3 (3級): reframe the existing library line — 「むかし、もうひとり、こえを だせる子が いた。…きみと、おなじだ。」 re-pointed from きみ to アイラ (サイレント was once a kid like きみ). Bookmark #3 = "and the whole".
- Ch.4 (準2級): the frozen "silence-quarrel" reveals the historical fact that words once hurt so much the town BEGGED for quiet — the silence was briefly WANTED. Bookmark #4 = "grey world".
- Ch.5 (準2級プラス/橋): the LAST DECREE — 「インクは あたらしいのに、じが ふるえている。だれかが、ずっと ひとりだ。」 (humanises the fresh-ink riddle). Bookmark #5 = "turned to".
- Ch.6 (2級): the scholars'-debate inference — 「王と、はじめての はなしては、おなじ ひと。おふれが ひとりでに とどくのは、おくる 手が もう いないから。」 (the cognitive turn = B1/B2 inference as story). Bookmark #6 = "colour."
- Ch.7 (準1級): PAYOFF. The reading items ARE the final clues; the name アイラ is spoken; the LAST word/Bookmark #7 = アイラ's own name. Attach an additive onCorrect to pre1 encounter 16: "No other hand moves for the King — it is his own past, still running, while the person behind it went quiet. アイラ fell silent first."

CONTRADICTION RESOLUTIONS: (a) Name = アイラ (one canon), shown only at 準1級; "サイレン/Siren" retired as a separate name — used only as the common-noun phenomenon サイレント. (b) Macguffin = 声の石 (map) revealed AS storybook pages (story) — one object. (c) アイラ is the First SPEAKER AND first STORYTELLER (both drafts fused). (d) Companion roster reconciled in CAST output (drop both クワイエト-rival and any villain-foil; アヤ is a friend-foil, クワイエ a frightened-friend mirror).
result.cast:
RECURRING CAST — every district threads the SAME people with grade-gated arcs, so it never reads as a flat parade of one-off NPCs. Each carries a personal silence that only resolves when the child's 英検 has grown enough to give the word back: the child's progress = the literal key to a friend's heart. 英検 items stay byte-identical; only WHO frames them changes.

1) スラ — THE COMPANION / きみ's MIRROR (deepened from existing 5級#1, 3級#1, pre1#6).
Concept: スラ is always ONE step behind the player — it attempts the grade きみ just cleared, says the new word WRONG first, and きみ's correct answer TEACHES it (protégé effect = strongest kid-retention mechanic). Born from the last drop of colour that survived サイレント; non-gendered, soft 「ぼく」. Personal silence = it never had a NAME.
VOICE (TTS): "young child, gentle, breathy, wonder, slight wobble, +30% pitch, 0.85x speed." 5級 it can only say "…ぷる?"; by 準1級 full emotional sentences.
ARC (colour-band schedule, wire to each town's cleared beat): 5級 +blue (word: Hello) / 4級 +green / 3級 +yellow / 準2 +orange / 2級 +red / 準1 full rainbow, says a paragraph, cries colour. Hidden fear (revealed 3級): if words stop, it goes grey and forgets きみ — so practising = keeping スラ's colour alive. PAYOFF (準1級): player NAMES スラ (pick 1 of 3 English-root names: Iris / Echo / Hue); スラ delivers the final Bookmark and its first complex sentence — 「ぼくに、なまえが ある… ありがとう、コトバたんてい。」 Its 3級 fear resolves into the season thesis: 「サイレントも、むかしは こえが ほしかっただけ なのかも。」

2) ことのは / Kotonoha — THE MENTOR (the Voiceless Librarian). Keeper of the 灰色の書庫.
Concept: she CANNOT SPEAK — she was first to lose her voice, given willingly to save the children's words. Mentors entirely through WRITTEN English the child DECODES (so she doubles as the reading-mentor) + page-turn/wind-chime SFX, optionally read aloud by スラ. She is the moral heart — insists サイレント is to be HEARD, not fought; appears at each district's ENTRY (sets the chapter theme) and at the boss gate (restates the no-battle contract). Personal silence = she can no longer remember the SOUND of her own name.
FRAMING JOB: owns READING/WRITING/opinion items — 長文 paraphrase, Eメール返信, 意見論述. Frame: 「手紙は、こたえを かえさなければ、あいてを しずけさに のこす。」 / "A letter must answer back, or it leaves the other in silence." 5級 entry theme: "Every word you say turns the grey to colour. Begin."
VOICE: SILENT all season (text only) — this is the design: her restored voice is the season's final gift. ONE spoken line, 準1級 only, rich TTS "adult woman, calm, warm, resonant, unhurried, slight emotion, 0.95x speed": to サイレント — 「おまえの こえも、まだ ここに ある。おかえり。」 / "Your voice, too, is still here. Welcome home." (Unlocked because きみ, having learned to read, speaks ことのは's written name aloud, returning her voice.)

3) 時計じい / Old Tic — THE ANCHOR NPC (recurring shopkeeper/guide, every district).
Concept: サイレント stopped TIME (a town with no words has no "tomorrow"); きみ's restored words restart each town clock. Tiny round clockmaker, brass ear-trumpet (half-deaf → a lovely in-world reason the child must ARTICULATE clearly). Calls きみ「たんていさん」. Carries a pocket-watch stopped since サイレント came. Personal silence = a portrait of someone whose name he's forgotten (his late wife).
FRAMING JOB: every TENSE/TIME item (was/were, will, present perfect, 過去分詞, since/for). Reframes "Where were you yesterday?" as 「じいの とけいが きのうを わすれてしまった。きのうの ことばを ひとつ、おしえてくれんか?」 — answer 'were' UNCHANGED; act = winding the clock back one day.
VOICE: "elderly man, warm, gravelly, slow, kind, occasional soft chuckle, -20% pitch, 0.9x speed" ("ほっほ").
ARC: sepia-grey, frozen mid-motion; each district restores one number to his watch face. PAYOFF (準1級, gated behind a relative-clause encounter): he forms a relative-clause memory ("the woman who taught me to mend clocks") and remembers her name — クロカ; watch ticks, floods to brass-and-blue, ear-trumpet falls away — 「たんていさん… きこえる。とけいが、また うごいておる。」 The season's quietest tear-jerker, unlocked BY GRAMMAR, not luck.

4) アヤ / Aya — THE FOIL (rival-FRIEND, never a bully — Layton has none).
Concept: a second child word-detective a year ahead, leaving chalk-clues that are actually scaffolds. Lets the game teach without a teacher. ~12, red scarf (the one colour she kept by will), worn notebook of solved ナゾ. Secretly lonely — being "ahead" meant being alone. Calls きみ「ルーキー」 (teasing→genuine). Personal silence = she's forgotten how to say "friend" (never had to).
FRAMING JOB: CHALLENGE/ORDERING items (語句整序/並びかえ) + harder reading-paraphrase traps, set as dares — 「ふふ、これ ならべられる? わたしは 3びょうで といたけど。」 then high-fives.
VOICE: "older child ~12, bright, confident, quick, playful, crisp, +10% pitch, 1.05x speed."
ARC: greying everywhere but the scarf. At 2級 she confesses 「ひとりで とくのは、しずかすぎた。だから ヒントを のこしてたんだ。」 (colour rushes back when きみ once corrects HER). PAYOFF (準1級): stands WITH きみ before サイレント — two voices, not one — her first full-colour word = "friend."

5) クワイエ / Quie — THE FRIGHTENED-FRIEND MIRROR (replaces the dropped クワイエト-villain). Non-gendered child who chose silence on purpose because their words once hurt their little sibling — states サイレント's belief child-to-child at 準2級: 「ことばは、ひとを きずつける。だから、ない ほうが いいんだ。」 Arc resolves at 2級: the "debate" is reframed — きみ doesn't DEFEAT Quie, きみ gives Quie the precise B2 words to finally APOLOGISE aloud to the sibling who came looking; Quie then joins the party for the last stretch. Quie = a small, child-safe rehearsal of アイラ's whole tragedy, healed early so the finale lands.

6) きみ — CHARACTERISATION VIA CHOICES. Stays non-gendered, no name, no birthright (power = phonics/英検). At each boss gate + companion beat, a 2-option TONE micro-choice that does NOT change the 英検 answer: 「やさしく / gently」("…こわくないよ。だいじょうぶ。") vs 「まっすぐ / boldly」("わたしは はなせる。きえない。"). Persist a tonePreference; the cast reacts — アヤ warms faster to bold, ことのは smiles at gentle, スラ mirrors きみ's tone as it relearns. The child authors きみ's personality while every answer stays byte-identical. Through-line: the quiet kid who turns out to hold the one thing the world lost — a voice — and spends it giving others theirs.

CAST-MYSTERY RHYME: 時計じい's frozen time, ことのは's willing sacrifice, アヤ's loneliness, and クワイエ's fear-of-own-words all RHYME with アイラ's forgotten loneliness — so the finale (the cast gives サイレント its voice back, not defeats it) feels earned by everyone's arc converging.
result.districts:
7 DISTRICTS = 7 英検 grades = 7 rings of the first word. Each: identity / palette-accent (within the ONE storybook-watercolour bible) / opening hook / restoration payoff (声の石=Bookmark). All names KEPT from code (in-world subtitle added). Hope escalates: lonely → guarded → frozen quarrel → サイレント resting → colour returning → colour floods.

CH.1 — 5級 〈はじまりの村 / Hushfield〉 (code name: ことばを失った村). RING-TAG: なまえと おと. THE RING OF NAMES & SOUNDS — the oldest, simplest words (a sound, a name, a greeting). This is where きみ learns the SPELL itself (phonics SATPIN, already built). PALETTE: ember-amber (one banked coal; each word lights an ember until the hearth blazes). HOOK (灰守セル): 「ここでは、みんな ひとつの おとしか だせない。…でも きみは、まだ きこえるんだね。」 / "Here we each have only one sound left. …But you can still hear, can't you?" MINI-MYSTERY: 「なぜ かまどの ひは きえないの?」 → Cel keeps it lit, waiting for a listener: 「まだ きける ひとを、まっていたんだ。」 PAYOFF: hearth blazes, スラ says "Hello!", Bookmark #1 ("Once, I") — Cel: 「これは、サイレントが いちばん さいしょに かいた おはなしの 1ページ目。しずけさは、むかし やさしい ひとだった。」 + Clue #1 (silence came centre→edge).

CH.2 — 4級 〈かざぐるま街 / Windmere〉 (code: 風の街). RING-TAG: まいにち. THE RING OF DAYS — everyday-life words (cook, buy, rain, yesterday); the wind literally blows small words off people's tongues mid-sentence. PALETTE: sky-cyan + windmill-white (the sails catch words and spin them back into air). HOOK (a child chasing a word-leaf): 「ことばが、つかまえても つかまえても とんでいっちゃう!」 MINI-MYSTERY: 「ことばは どこへ かぜに はこばれるの?」 → gathered, not destroyed; the sails turn toward the distant Grey Square. PAYOFF: the great windmill turns the right way, daily chatter returns; Bookmark #2 ("told a story,") caught in the sails, read aloud by a braver スラ; Clue #2 (motive = mercy, reusing Sura's existing line).

CH.3 — 3級 〈ものがたりの都 / Tellgate〉 (code: 学びの都). RING-TAG: ものがたり. THE RING OF STORIES — school/travel/narration words (because/when, present perfect "have you ever…"). Restoring a word completes a story on a page. PALETTE: parchment-gold + ink-blue. INTRODUCES ことのは (here the 大図書館 surfaces) and クワイエ (the hooded child who shuts a book when きみ speaks). MINI-MYSTERY: 「なぜ 大図書館の まんなかの 本だけ しろい?」 → it is サイレント/アイラ's own storybook, its pages torn into the 7 bookmarks. PAYOFF: people tell their own stories again; Bookmark #3 ("and the whole"); the OLD-BOOK clue re-pointed to アイラ; Quie, for the first time, does NOT close the book.

CH.4 — 準2級 〈つばさ港 / Tsubasa Harbour〉 (code: 社会の港町). RING-TAG: みんなの こえ. THE RING OF VOICES-TOGETHER — opinion/society words ("I think…", problems & solutions); the emotional heart of WHY アイラ did it — the town that once BEGGED for silence. PALETTE: harbour-teal + sail-crimson. HOOK: two merchants frozen mid-soundless-quarrel; クワイエ states サイレント's belief — 「ことばは、ひとを きずつける。だから、ない ほうが いいんだ。」 MINI-MYSTERY: 「なぜ ふたりの しょうにんは、こえも なく にらみあったまま?」 → they lost the words for "I disagree, but I still respect you." PAYOFF: returned words let them reconcile and shake hands; ships catch colour and sail; Bookmark #4 ("grey world"); Quie, shaken that words HEALED, follows きみ; Clue #4 (silence was briefly wanted).

CH.5 — 準2級プラス 〈きりのかけはし / The Mist Bridge〉 (code: 試練の橋, the "2-year wall"). RING-TAG: ながい はし. THE RING-GAP — NOT a town: a single immense fog-bridge IS the chapter. Dense, abstract, long reading; the loneliest, most beautiful Layton-melancholy chapter. PALETTE: lantern-amber in white fog (each solved ナゾ lights the next lantern; only by lit lanterns can きみ see the far side). NPCs: a lantern-keeper who's turned back many times; ことのは/Cel mid-bridge to steady きみ; FIRST whole sight of サイレント — resting, not blocking — 「ここまで きたの。…つかれたら、すわっても いいんだよ。」 MINI-MYSTERY: 「はしの まんなかに すわっている、はいいろの ひとは だれ?」 PAYOFF: all lanterns lit, the far castle-town appears through clearing mist; Bookmark #5 ("turned to"); the trembling unfinished LAST DECREE found (Clue #5); サイレント rises and walks AHEAD toward the Grey Square — leading, not fleeing.

CH.6 — 2級 〈がくしゃの じょうかまち / Loreward〉 (code: 学者の城下町). RING-TAG: りゆう. THE RING OF REASONS — argument/logic/business connectives (however, moreover, therefore); restoring words restores people's ability to REASON together. PALETTE: violet + brass. クワイエ's arc RESOLVES here. MINI-MYSTERY: 「クワイエは、だれに あやまりたいの?」 → きみ gives Quie the B2 words to apologise to the sibling who came looking; the apology is answered. PAYOFF: Quie joins for the last stretch; Bookmark #6 ("colour."); through the scholars' debate きみ INFERS Clue #6 — the King and the First Speaker are the same person — assembling the truth they can't yet say. Now 6 bookmarks + the start-pendant page = near-complete sentence; only the LAST word (アイラ's name) is missing, and only サイレント holds it.

CH.7 — 準1級 〈灰色のひろば / The Grey Square〉 (code name KEPT). RING-TAG: はじまり. THE FIRST RING / THE SOURCE — where the first word was spoken; fully grey; アイラ sits at the centre. NOT a boss arena — a quiet, sorrowful homecoming (no throne, no siege, no crown). PALETTE: full-spectrum — the only district where ALL colour returns at once, flooding outward from アイラ as the story is read back, then racing along the Mist Bridge through every healed district. The whole recovered cast gathers (スラ delivers Bookmark #7 + its first full sentence; ことのは, 時計じい, アヤ, クワイエ). FINALE (reframed): きみ does NOT out-argue/defeat — the existing 準1級 items become "help きみ read the next clause of サイレント's story correctly" (same choices/correctIndex). The name アイラ is spoken; the LAST word returned = アイラ's own name. アイラ: 「…ありがとう。わたしも、また はなして いいのかな。」 ことのは speaks her one line: 「おまえの こえも、まだ ここに ある。おかえり。」 The title ことばの勇者 is shown as the COUNT of voices returned — including サイレント's.

LEAKAGE FIX (mandatory, flavour-only, choices/correctIndex/distractors UNCHANGED): pre1 enc.1 "the capital is under siege … one born to rule … the gate opens" → reflavour to Grey-Square mist-clearing + a non-royal "the way opens." pre1 cleared 「あなたの旅が、世界を救った / saved the world」 → 「あなたが、せかいに ──そして サイレントに── ことばの かえしかたを おもいださせた / you reminded the world, and the Silence, how to give words back.」 After editing, run repo-wide grep 王|城|玉座|救った|siege|rule|crown across quest_data.dart to confirm no conquest/royalty residue remains.
result.nazoPattern:
ナゾ-FRAMING PATTERN — the WHY-WRAP, never the WHAT-CHANGE. A dry 英検 item answers a grammar/vocab question for its own sake; a ナゾ answers a WORLD question whose only correct key happens to be that exact choice. The item (stem, blank, 4 options, key) stays BYTE-IDENTICAL. We add three things AROUND it, never inside:
(1) こわれているもの — a broken thing causally tied to the grammar point (a stopped clock, a name that won't say, a colour that fell out, a torn label).
(2) 探偵メモ — きみ notices the clue; this is where the grammar's MEANING lives, stated as evidence, never as a rule.
(3) onCorrect (restoration) — the thing mends; a colour/voice returns; サイレント loosens by one word. Never "正解!", always "なおった".

THE REUSABLE 5-STEP CARD (apply to all ~155 items; the 4 options + key are COPY-PASTED from the 英検 DB, never edited):
STEP 1 — Name the grammar's real-world JOB. (since/for = pinning a START vs measuring a LENGTH; who/which = a person vs a thing we point at; a/an = picking the right SHAPE of a thing; past tense = it already happened, behind us.)
STEP 2 — Find the broken object whose fixing IS that job. (duration/tense→clock/candle/footprint; plural→basket of fruit/coins; a/an/the→a key that fits one lock; relative who/which→a witness pointing; conditional/modal→a bridge that appears only IF; vocab/collocation→a faded sign/torn recipe.)
STEP 3 — Write the 探偵メモ (JA, furigana): state the EVIDENCE pointing to the answer's meaning WITHOUT naming the answer — reasoning about the world, not a worksheet.
STEP 4 — Keep the 英検 stem VERBATIM as the object's own words: npcLine = the item stem unchanged; choices = the 4 official options in order; correctIndex = the official key.
STEP 5 — Write the restoration onCorrect tied to that NPC's tiny arc.

HINT-LADDER (3 rungs, calm スラ/Layton tone; the key word NEVER appears at any rung; rung 3 teaches the DISTINCTION, child still chooses):
rung1 みなおし: 「{clueObject}を もういちど みてみよう。」
rung2 いみ: the semantic contrast restated as a question (no answer word).
rung3 きまり: the rule as a detective's principle — "{rule} {clueObject}は どっち だと いってる?"
Wrong tap (NEVER "ちがう!"/red flash): スラ tilts head — 「んー、サイレントが まだ ここに いるね。もういちど、いっしょに。」 then offers the next rung.

READY EXAMPLES:
EX1 (FLAGSHIP, 3級 since/for, とまった時計のナゾ) — NPC 時計じい. 探偵メモ: 「とけいは『3じ』で とまってる。それって『3じから ずっと』? それとも『3じかん だけ』? ことばが きまれば、はりが うごく。」 npcLine: 'The clock has been stopped ___ three o'clock.' choices ['since','for','from','at'] key 0. onCorrect: 「カチ…コチ…! はりが うごきだした。『3じから ずっと…まってたんだ。ありがとう、たんてい。』 とおりに 金いろの 光が もどる。」
EX2 (5級 a/an, existing line 608 REUSED) — NPC スラ. npcLine 'Are you hungry? Here, this is ___ apple.' choices ['a','an','the','some'] key 'an'. メモ+restoration ADDED only: 「シャキッ! りんごが まっ赤に いろづいた。『apple は あいうえおの おと だから an なんだね!』」
EX3 (準2級 who/which, 目撃者のナゾ) — NPC ノア. npcLine 'I am looking for the person ___ took the statue's voice.' choices ['who','which','where','what'] key 'who'. メモ: 「なくなったのは ひと? もの? ひとを さすか ものを さすかで ことばが ちがう。」 onCorrect: ぞうの ほほに いろが さす。
EX4 (4級 past tense, もどらない足あと) — NPC ベラ. npcLine 'Yesterday, I ___ to the old tower.' choices ['walked','walk','walking','walks'] key 'walked'. メモ: 「ゆきに あしあと。いま あるく じゃない、もう あるいた あと。おわった ことの ことばは?」 onCorrect: あしあとが きえる、さかみちに あおが もどる。
EX5 (2級 collocation, やぶれたレシピ) — NPC ミナ. npcLine 'Please ___ a decision before the festival begins.' choices ['make','take','do','have'] key 'make'. メモ: 「ことばには なかよしの あいてが いる。decision と くっつくのは?」 onCorrect: なべから ゆげ、いろが もどる。
EX6 (準1級 conditional, あらわれない橋 — THE CLIMAX) — NPC サイレント (half-voiced). npcLine 'If I ___ my voice back, I would finally say sorry.' choices ['had','have','will have','having'] key 'had'. メモ: 「サイレントが はじめて ことばを さがしてる。ありえない いまを ことばに する かたちは?」 onCorrect: ひかりの はしが かかる。「…ごめんね。そして、ありがとう。」 せかいに いろが もどる。(restoration-not-conquest finale.)

ANTI-CHEAT / SAFETY RAILS (feed to the content-qa gate): (1) npcLine == 英検 DB stem byte-for-byte. (2) choices == official 4 options, same ORDER. (3) correctIndex == official key. (4) NO frame line may eliminate a distractor or name the answer — frame the STEM's meaning, never narrow the OPTIONS → REJECT. (5) サイレント NEVER personified as enemy, no slaying verbs ("つめたい/かなしい/まだ ここに いる" OK; "わるい" NOT) → REJECT. (6) onCorrect restores colour/voice, no "正解"/score language. (7) furigana matches the district's grade (ひらがな-first 5–4級; kanji+furigana from 3級 up).
result.buildFirst:
BUILD/AUTHOR FIRST — the 5級 district (town_eiken5, はじまりの村 / Hushfield) as the immediate deepened slice. It is the lowest-risk, highest-leverage proof: it already has the phonics scaffold and スラ in code, so deepening it ships the cast+ナゾ+mystery pattern end-to-end for the whole season to copy. Author ONLY framing/メモ/restoration/cleared text — touch NO choices/correctIndex/distractors.

DATA-MODEL PREP (do once, before authoring): add two OPTIONAL fields to QuestTown so existing literals stay valid — `String? wordBookmark` (the 声の石-as-page line) and an ArrivalScene trio `arrivalHookEn/arrivalHookJa + clearedHopeBeat`. Add a persisted `tonePreference` (gently|boldly) read at boss gates. None are required; all default null/neutral so the other 6 towns compile untouched.

5級 AUTHORING CHECKLIST (in town_eiken5):
1. ARRIVAL HOOK (灰守セル): 「ここでは、みんな ひとつの おとしか だせない。…でも きみは、まだ きこえるんだね。」 / "Here we each have only one sound left. …But you can still hear, can't you?" (play existing audio/phonics/phoneme_s.mp3).
2. ことのは ENTRY THEME (silent text + page-turn SFX): "Every word you say turns the grey to colour. Begin." (introduce the reading-mentor here even before 3級, as a wordless presence.)
3. スラ WRONG→RIGHT keyword beat (reusable QuestStep pattern): スラ says the district keyword wrong ("…Heddo?"); the child's correct answer teaches it; onCorrect: 「スラが『Hello!』と いえた! あおい いろが、ひとつ もどった。」 (= スラ's first colour band, blue.)
4. HEARTH MINI-MYSTERY onCorrect (new flavour on an existing teach step): ナゾ 「なぜ かまどの ひは きえないの?」 → Cel: 「まだ きける ひとを、まっていたんだ。」
5. CLUE #1 (assign to おばあさん/セル, after ~encounter 6): 「この しずけさは、そとから きたんじゃない。まんなかから、あるいて きたんだよ。」 (establishes centre→edge direction — the mystery's first thread).
6. RE-FRAME the existing 5級 a/an item (line 608) and plural -s item (line 618) with EX2/EX7 メモ+restoration only — proof the WHY-WRAP works on byte-identical items.
7. BOSS-GATE TONE CHOICE (does NOT change the 英検 answer): 「やさしく」("…こわくないよ。だいじょうぶ。") vs 「まっすぐ」("わたしは はなせる。きえない。") — persist tonePreference.
8. CLEARED BEAT + BOOKMARK #1: extend the existing cleared line — Cel presses Bookmark #1 ("Once, I") into きみ's hand: 「これは、サイレントが いちばん さいしょに かいた おはなしの 1ページ目。しずけさは、むかし やさしい ひとだった。」 スラ gains its blue band.

GATE BEFORE COMMIT (per CLAUDE.md governance): every authored line passes the content-qa subagent against the ANTI-CHEAT/SAFETY rails (npcLine byte-identical, options/order/correctIndex untouched, no distractor-eliminating frame, サイレント never an enemy, onCorrect = restoration not score, furigana = grade). Then `flutter analyze` (0) + `flutter test` (0 failures). Spec-freeze: this bible is the approved spec — do NOT re-change サイレント=アイラ / 声の石=pages / no-battle finale mid-build; escalate to CEO before any structural change.

SEQUENCING AFTER THE SLICE: (1) run the LEAKAGE FIX on pre1 (under-siege/born-to-rule/世界を救った → restoration wording; grep 王|城|玉座|救った|siege|rule|crown) so the finale matches the new canon; (2) propagate the 4-anchor recurrence map (時計じい=TIME/grammar+clock beat / アヤ=challenge-ordering dare / ことのは=reading-writing theme+boss reminder / スラ=keyword wrong→right + colour gain) and one clue-beat per remaining town 4級→準1級, splitting the 7 bookmarks in order ("Once, I"/"told a story,"/"and the whole"/"grey world"/"turned to"/"colour."/アイラ's name); (3) author the Mist Bridge (準2プラス) as a single-location chapter and convert the 準1級 items into "read the next clause of サイレント's story." Build edge→centre, mirroring both difficulty and the mystery's reveal order.
